title: French government admits 'failure' and blames judicial system over death of 11-year-old girl
subhead: The government does not intend to be the only one bearing responsibility for the death of Lyhanna, a girl who had been missing for a week before her body was found on a farm.

title: 'No one was prepared for what was about to happen': Gaza flotilla activist speaks about violence she suffered in Israel
Russian player Andreeva wins French Open
French telecom giants reach deal to acquire struggling rival SFR
subhead: The companies – Bouygues Telecom, Orange and Free owner Iliad – said SFR parent Altice had accepted their offer of €20.35 billion for the struggling mobile operator. The deal could reshape the French telecoms landscape by reducing the number of main operators from four to three.

title: Mélenchon hopes Saint-Denis rally will turn into show of strength
subhead: Radical-left leader Jean-Luc Mélenchon will hold his first major campaign meeting on Sunday, in the northern Paris suburb of Saint-Denis. He hopes the event's success will pressure some on the left to rally behind his candidacy.

title: With 'De Gaulle: Tilting Iron,' Simon Abkarian boldly steps into the General's shoes
subhead: The first part of a two-part film directed by Antonin Baudry, this movie encompasses every possible register: war, action, love, politics, humor, lyricism and grandeur.

title: Sagrada Familia organist to lead landmark Mass as Pope Leo XIV visits Spain
subhead: The pontiff is expected in Barcelona on June 10 for the inauguration of the monumental main tower of the basilica envisioned by Antoni Gaudí. Chief organist Juan de la Rubia is in charge of the musical repertoire for the ceremony.

Over three decades, China's promise became a trap for European industry
subhead: 'How China is Devouring Europe' (1/4). In just a few decades, China has gone from being the world's factory to a major technological rival for Europeans, who had hoped to profit on China's vast domestic market but underestimated its capacity for innovation.
byline: Bastien Bonnefous,  Isabelle Chaperon and  Sophie Fay

title: Zelensky's letter to Putin is actually addressed to Russia's elites who are tired of the war in Ukraine
subhead: Behind the offer of an outstretched hand, the Ukrainian president's letter to the Russian leader seeks to exploit Moscow's vulnerabilities and the Russian people's exhaustion after four years of high-intensity war.
byline: Benjamin Quénelle,  Claire Gatinois and  Thomas d’Istria

title: HSBC charged for helping former Lebanese central bank head embezzle hundreds of millions
subhead: The Swiss subsidiary of HSBC is suspected of having helped former Lebanese central bank chief Riad Salameh embezzle more than $300 million from the country's central bank. The charges mark a significant development in the French judicial investigation.
